# Fixture cycle: solo-engineer

A canned 12-capture cycle simulating a solo engineer's morning of work
on a Rust CLI tool, PR review, and blog drafting.

## Files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `manifest.json` | Replay parameters (top_n, ceiling, thresholds, models) |
| `captures.json` | 12 synthetic capture rows across 3 work sessions |
| `user-profile.md` | Solo-engineer profile injected into synthesis prompt |
| `tier-definitions.json` | Standard 3-tier rubric with default weights |
| `prior-dispositions.json` | 2 prior disposed recommendations for dedup testing |
| `anthropic/<hash>.json` | Recorded Anthropic responses keyed by request hash |
| `output.json` | Generated by `replay-cycle` (gitignored) |

## How to run

```bash
cargo run --bin replay-cycle -- --fixture-dir tests/fixtures/cycle-solo-engineer
```

If the `anthropic/` directory is missing or empty, `FixtureClient` runs
in strict mode and errors out with the exact request hashes the pipeline
needs. Use `pipeline_replay` integration test (which runs against
`MockAnthropicClient` with hand-crafted responses) for end-to-end
coverage that doesn't depend on recorded fixtures.

## Recording fixtures

In Week 4, after the live Anthropic client is wired and tested, run the
binary once against the live API with a tiny test cycle and capture the
responses. The `record-fixtures` flag (TODO) will write each response to
`anthropic/<hash>.json` automatically.

Until then: edit `pipeline_replay.rs` to use `MockAnthropicClient` with
canned responses and validate the pipeline end-to-end.
